debateOfTheWeek.hook: Rice, pork, and pure South Louisiana pride — who really owns the crown this week?

seasonalRotation.note: When the heat hits, the flavor wars start.

items.description: Smoked, steamed, spicy, or classic — every parish swears theirs is best.

contenders.caseFor: Consistent links with rich seasoning and that perfect snap. It's the benchmark people compare everyone else to.
Balanced spice, juicy texture, and cracklins that hit every time. A road-trip stop that's worth the detour.
Flavor-forward and a little bolder than average. If you like your boudin with attitude, this is your move.
Underrated and climbing fast with locals. Great value and a no-frills vibe that feels old-school Cajun.

items.description: Crunchy outside, melt-in-the-middle fat, and the eternal question: salty or pepper-hot?

contenders.caseFor: Golden crunch and reliable seasoning. People grab an extra bag before they even leave the parking lot.
Some call it unbeatable, others say too rich — which is exactly why this debate gets loud at every boil.
Peppery and intense in the best way. Not for the timid, but pure joy for heat lovers.

items.description: Spice level, peel quality, and price per pound — this one can start a family feud.

contenders.caseFor: Strong boil flavor and steady quality through peak season. A go-to when you don't want to gamble.
Legendary local reputation and serious flavor depth. This is where crawfish memories get made.
Big portions, solid heat, and improving every season. The buzz keeps growing.

items.description: Warm, boozy sauce or classic sweet cream? The spoon battles are real.

contenders.caseFor: Rich custard body with a sauce that'll make you scrape the bowl.
Decadent but balanced — sweet without going overboard.

items.description: Icing ratio, filling style, and who gets the baby — Acadiana takes this personally.

contenders.caseFor: Classic style done right with pillowy dough and clean cinnamon flavor.
Creative spins and fun fillings that still respect tradition.

items.description: Creamy vs chunky, grape or no grape — this gets surprisingly competitive.

contenders.caseFor: Fresh, balanced, and perfect on flaky croissants.
Dependable and crowd-pleasing for quick lunch runs.

items.description: Peppered strips and smoky chew — glovebox essentials for every road trip.

contenders.caseFor: Robust seasoning and serious smoke depth.
Classic texture with just enough heat.

items.description: From backyard pits to game day grills, this is the hero link.

contenders.caseFor: Excellent snap and seasoning that holds up on the grill.
A bolder blend for folks who want more kick.

items.description: Dark roux loyalty runs deep — this debate can last all winter.

contenders.caseFor: Rich roux and deep Cajun flavor in every spoon.
Hearty portions with a soulful, old-school finish.

items.description: Silky gravy, rice ratio, and spice balance — judged hard by locals.

contenders.caseFor: Classic crawfish flavor and smooth texture that never misses.
Some call it the best in town, others prefer thicker gravy — so the argument lives on.

items.description: Crunch factor, juiciness, and seasoning in the crust — no room for mediocrity.

contenders.caseFor: Unexpected sleeper pick with serious crisp and flavor.
Beloved by many, questioned by purists — exactly the kind of debate fuel we need.

items.description: Rice, gravy, and two sides that hug your soul.

contenders.caseFor: Portions are huge and every tray feels like a Sunday kitchen special.
Reliable comfort classics and neighborhood charm.
